query: pick a rhythm, rest a finger on your Mac's trackpad, and follow an orb that expands and contracts review

results.title: OpenAI wants your launches
results.content:
## Press down and breathe

Dvain (@dvain) wanted a reset ritual that didn't mean picking up his phone, so he built Breva: pick a rhythm, rest a finger on your Mac's trackpad, and follow an orb that expands and contracts while the trackpad pulses with every inhale and exhale. Local, no account, no subscription. [...] 🔥 Our Take: Your trackpad has had a haptic engine since 2015, and in ten years its only job has been faking the feel of a click. Breva is the first app we've seen use it for something human: the Mac physically breathes under your finger. Meditation apps live on your phone, the same device serving you the stress. Putting the two-minute reset inside the machine you work on, one keystroke from whatever spiked your heart rate, is the right placement. One commenter said it feels "like the mac itself is breathing with you." That's the whole product, and it's enough.

•13 reviews

I've been using BetterTouchTool for over a decade, and it's undoubtedly the most essential application in my daily workflow. I have dozens of custom finger gestures configured for various tasks, using it to trigger shortcuts in different applications. It provides a much faster way to execute actions compared to memorizing and typing keyboard shortcuts. Without question, BetterTouchTool is one of my most crucial productivity tools - I genuinely wouldn't know how to work efficiently without it.

1 view

This is the best productivity tool I've ever used. Very powerful keyboard macros, including thoughtful trackpad gestures, stapled to window snapping and other strong features. If I had to ditch everything else in my setup, this would be my one to keep.

Kleanly

Kleanly

Maker

📌

It started with a frustrating experience we've all had - trying to clean my MacBook keyboard while the screen was on. Every swipe sent random characters flying or triggered unwanted shortcuts. Even with the screen off, the keyboard remained active. I knew there had to be a better way. That's why I created Kleanly - a simple menubar app that lets you clean your Mac without turning it off. One click disables your keyboard and trackpad completely, letting you wipe away dirt and dust worry-free. When you're done, just double-tap the trackpad to resume. No more accidental messages, no more random shortcuts - just a clean Mac and peace of mind. Sometimes the simplest solutions make the biggest difference. That's Kleanly.

Report
